   313→// ============================================================================
   314→// Public dispatch API

   317→/// Dispatch JJK commands from raw arguments.
   318→///
   319→/// Called by VOK when it receives an external subcommand starting with "jjx_".
   320→/// The first element of `args` should be the subcommand name (e.g., "jjx_muster").
   321→///
   322→/// Returns exit code (0 for success, non-zero for failure).
   323→pub fn jjrx_dispatch(args: &[OsString]) -> i32 {
   324→    // Prepend synthetic binary name for clap parsing.
   325→    // Clap expects args[0] to be the binary name (used in help text).
   326→    // VOK passes ["jjx_muster", ...] so we prepend "jjx" to get ["jjx", "jjx_muster", ...].
   327→    let mut full_args = vec![OsString::from("jjx")];
   328→    full_args.extend(args.iter().cloned());
   329→
   330→    // Parse the subcommand and arguments
   331→    let parsed = match jjrx_JjxCommands::try_parse_from(&full_args) {
   332→        Ok(cmd) => cmd,
   333→        Err(e) => {
   334→            // Let clap handle help/version/error display
   335→            e.print().ok();
   336→            return if e.kind() == clap::error::ErrorKind::DisplayHelp
   337→                   || e.kind() == clap::error::ErrorKind::DisplayVersion {
   338→                0
   339→            } else {
   340→                1
   341→            };
   342→        }
   343→    };
   344→
   345→    match parsed {
   346→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Notch(args) => zjjrx_run_notch(args),
   347→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Chalk(args) => zjjrx_run_chalk(args),
   348→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Rein(args) => zjjrx_run_rein(args),
   349→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Validate(args) => zjjrx_run_validate(args),
   350→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Muster(args) => zjjrx_run_muster(args),
   351→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Saddle(args) => zjjrx_run_saddle(args),
   352→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Parade(args) => zjjrx_run_parade(args),
   353→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Retire(args) => zjjrx_run_retire(args),
   354→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Nominate(args) => zjjrx_run_nominate(args),
   355→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Slate(args) => zjjrx_run_slate(args),
   356→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Rail(args) => zjjrx_run_rail(args),
   357→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Tally(args) => zjjrx_run_tally(args),
   358→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Draft(args) => zjjrx_run_draft(args),
   359→    }
   360→}

   362→/// Check if a command name is a JJK command
   363→pub fn jjrx_is_jjk_command(name: &str) -> bool {
   364→    name.starts_with("jjx_")
   365→}

   371→fn zjjrx_run_notch(args: jjrx_NotchArgs) -> i32 {
   372→    let coronet = match Coronet::jjrf_parse(&args.coronet) {
   373→        Ok(c) => c,
   374→        Err(e) => {
   375→            eprintln!("jjx_notch: error: {}", e);
   376→            return 1;
   377→        }
   378→    };
   379→
   380→    let prefix = format_notch_prefix(&coronet);
   381→
   382→    let commit_args = vvc::vvcc_CommitArgs {
   383→        prefix: Some(prefix),
   384→        message: None,
   385→        allow_empty: false,
   386→        no_stage: false,
   387→    };
   388→
   389→    vvc::commit(&commit_args)
   390→}
   391→
   392→fn zjjrx_run_chalk(args: jjrx_ChalkArgs) -> i32 {
   393→    let marker = match ChalkMarker::jjrn_parse(&args.marker) {
   394→        Ok(m) => m,
   395→        Err(e) => {
   396→            eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {}", e);
   397→            return 1;
   398→        }
   399→    };
   400→
   401→    // Try parsing as Coronet first (5 base64 chars), then as Firemark (2 base64 chars)
   402→    let identity = args.identity.strip_prefix('₢').or_else(|| args.identity.strip_prefix('₣')).unwrap_or(&args.identity);
   403→
   404→    let message = if identity.len() == 5 {
   405→        // Coronet - pace-level chalk
   406→        let coronet = match Coronet::jjrf_parse(&args.identity) {
   407→            Ok(c) => c,
   408→            Err(e) => {
   409→                eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {}", e);
   410→                return 1;
   411→            }
   412→        };
   413→        format_chalk_message(&coronet, marker, &args.description)
   414→    } else if identity.len() == 2 {
   415→        // Firemark - heat-level (discussion only)
   416→        if marker.jjrn_requires_pace() {
   417→            eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {} marker requires a Coronet (pace identity), not a Firemark", marker.jjrn_as_str());
   418→            return 1;
   419→        }
   420→        let firemark = match Firemark::jjrf_parse(&args.identity) {
   421→            Ok(fm) => fm,
   422→            Err(e) => {
   423→                eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {}", e);
   424→                return 1;
   425→            }
   426→        };
   427→        format_heat_discussion(&firemark, &args.description)
   428→    } else {
   429→        eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: identity must be Coronet (5 chars) or Firemark (2 chars), got {} chars", identity.len());
   430→        return 1;
   431→    };
   432→
   433→    let commit_args = vvc::vvcc_CommitArgs {
   434→        prefix: None,
   435→        message: Some(message),
   436→        allow_empty: true,
   437→        no_stage: true,
   438→    };
   439→
   440→    vvc::commit(&commit_args)
   441→}

   464→        }
   465→    };
   466→
   467→    match gallops.jjrg_validate() {

   469→            println!("Gallops validation passed");
   470→            0
   471→        }
   472→        Err(errors) => {
   473→            eprintln!("jjx_validate: validation failed with {} error(s):", errors.len());

   475→                eprintln!("  - {}", error);

   478→        }
   479→    }

   554→    use crate::jjrc_core::jjrc_timestamp_date;
